title: verilator --coverage producing bad .cpp with code:  0U = 0U;
body:
Thanks for taking the time to report this.

Can you attach an example that shows the issue?  (Must be openly licensed, completely self-contained and self-checking so can directly run what you provide. Ideally use test_regress format, see https://veripool.org/guide/latest/contributing.html?highlight=test_regress#reporting-bugs

> I will try, don't have a testcase I can share yet.

What 'verilator' command line do we use to run your example?

> long command, but essentially `verilator [--binary | --cc] --coverage`

What 'verilator --version' are you using?  Did you try it with the git master version?
> Verilator 5.033 devel rev v5.032-28-gbb871728c

What OS and distribution are you using?
> Ubuntu22.04

May we assist you in trying to fix this in Verilator yourself?
> Yes, but I first need to need a smaller distilled reproducible testcase first.

> Happens in my a function `V{{top}}..._eval_static__TOP`, where a bunch of values are assigned with `0U` in C++. A few it appears the left hand side didn't get resolved correctly, and so I get `0U = 0U;`  I suspect this is a struct, might be unpacked but not sure yet because is part of a big design. Also, it doesn't seem to affect all of my designs, just a few, and it's definitely in Testbench non-synthesizable code. SV-Interfaces are not involved.

Ideally a short term fix for me is - avoid this line `0U = 0U;` of .cpp entirely, even if it does affect coverage for this .sv line of code.

(Please avoid attaching screenshots that show text - you can convert images to text using e.g. https://ocr.space)
